title: The LED Robot and the Honey Tree of Cornell Street
summary: A hungry bear named Winnie the Pooh meets a crying baby robot made of electronics and LEDs. They team up to find a beehive on Cornell Street, using Pooh's ladder to reach a pot of honey. After sharing a sweet snack, they travel to the robot's colorful metal home, where the baby robot is reunited with her mama and dada robot.
audioFilename: * Stars and Stories - Pooh and Baby Robot.m4a

highlightQuote.text: I'm my snack right now, robot.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Um... sure, thank you.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: This is this.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hmm, what's it mixed with?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Tins and stuff, even radishes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Radishes in the chocolate? That's very interesting. Okay, we've got...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: So now it tastes yummy and brown.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Now we have some stars. What should we do a story about?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Addie Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: About Pooh Bear.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Pooh Bear, okay. Yeah. And what's Pooh Bear going to do in this story?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know. Keep telling the story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Keep telling the story and then we'll find out. Let's improvise the story together. Once upon a time, Winnie the Pooh was...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I also put some sugar and mustard and honey and salt in it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: In what?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: In this mixed thing of candy.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Um, that sounds yummy. Winnie the Pooh was very hungry and so he thought to himself, 'Hmm, I wonder what there is about to eat?' And he looked around his house, but he couldn't find anything that he wanted. And so he went out to see if he could find anyone else that had a snack that he could share. And who did he find?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: If you just listen and then who came trotting along? It was nobody.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was nobody. Oh, nobody came along. So he sat and listened a little bit longer. And then he heard a funny sound. What did he hear?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hmm, I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: He heard, 'Wah, wah, wah, wah, wah, wah'. What was that sound? He thought to himself.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hmm, it was just somebody crying.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Somebody crying... but they... it was somebody crying in a funny way, almost like a robot. And Winnie the Pooh thought to himself, perhaps it was, in fact, a baby robot. And he was correct, for just at that moment, stumbling into view came a tiny robot. What was the tiny robot made of?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was made of electronics and LEDs.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it was made of electronics and LEDs and it was saying 'Wah, wah, wah, wah'. And what did Winnie the Pooh say to it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm looking for some honey.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the baby robot said, 'Wah, wah, I'm hungry too. Maybe we could find a snack together. Wah, wah, wah'.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Could we have some honey maybe?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, we can have some honey.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Where is it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's at a beehive and it's the right sort of bees and it's on Cornell Street.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, how do we get there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You take the left and then you go the right.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, will you take me there please?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm my snack right now, robot.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What are you eating?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm eating mixed chocolate. Mmm.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What is the chocolate mixed with?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: All kinds of things.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Like what?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: May I try some, please?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, you may. Here you go.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Thank you. Mmm, mmm, mmm. Oh my gosh, is there mustard in that chocolate?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Okay, yes, it is.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And blueberries?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No blueberries in there, but I mixed a bunch of blueberries and some strawberries and maybe some... and I also mixed raspberries.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow. Now Pooh Bear, could you please help me find my mama robot?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, there's only honey left.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Could I have some honey please, too?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Thank you. Mmm, mmm, mmm.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hey, that's not the right sort of bees, that's the wrong sort of bees, so you must go on Cornell Street.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh dear, let's go there. And so Winnie the Pooh and the baby robot went together on Cornell Street and they found a very tall tree, and at the top of the tree they saw...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah. And now you're at the right place and they must make the wrong... the right sort of honey and you can eat it because no bees are there right now.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: But how will we get to the top of the tree? Said the baby robot to Winnie the Pooh. And what did Pooh Bear say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You got a stool... no, you got a ladder at your house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh! Winnie the Pooh said, 'Yes, I... I do happen to have a ladder at my house. Let me just run back and grab it.' And so Winnie the Pooh ran to his house and ran right back with a tall, tall ladder. And he said, 'Okay, now I'll put this up against the tree. Shall I go up or would you like to go up, baby robot?' And what did baby robot say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: First I want to go up, said the baby robot.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh okay, I'll hold the ladder for safety, said Winnie the Pooh. And the baby robot set about climbing up the ladder. Clink, clink, clink, clink, clink, clink, clink. And climbed quickly all the way to the top of the ladder. And what did she see there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She... she saw that there were no bees.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh thank goodness.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But then she could take the whole pot home.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, so she took the pot of honey and climbed back down the ladder. Clink, clink, clink, clink, clink, clink, clink. And she climbed down to the bottom where Winnie the Pooh was waiting. And what did Winnie the Pooh say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Thank you for getting my honey.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Um, and then the baby robot said, 'Let's eat a little bit right now'. And they both shared a little bit of the honey together. And it was delicious.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And there's just a little bit.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: But the baby robot was still a little bit sad and she said, 'Wah, wah, wah, wah'. And Winnie the Pooh said, 'Why are you crying, baby robot?' 'Well, now I would like to find my mama robot.' And Winnie the Pooh said, 'Oh, uh where is she?' And what did the baby robot say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She's at your house. She's pumping milk for you, baby robot.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, said baby robot, 'She's at my house'. Um, and so Winnie the Pooh and the baby robot went back to the robot family house. And what did the robot's house look like? What kind of a house did the robots live in?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A metal house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A metal house. And what color was the metal house?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Green and blue and indigo and violet, gray, blue... no, gray, tan and white and black and pink too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. And what kind of decorations did the robot's house have on it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It had no kind of decoration.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, none? Did it have windows?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, but it did have huge decorations.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Like what?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Leaf decorations.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What kind?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Leaf decorations. No, it's gnome decorations.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, gnome decorations. That sounds like a nice house. And so Winnie the Pooh brought baby robot back to her house, which was a beautiful big multi-colored metal house with leaf decorations and gnome decorations. And baby robot's mama and dada were very happy to see her. And Pooh Bear said, 'Okay, bye-bye everyone'. And that was the end of the story. All right, let's turn out the light. All right, now it's time to use the potty.

characters.name: Winnie the Pooh
characters.description: A hungry yet generous soul, this bear shares his honey and ladder with Hattie on their quest for the perfect golden harvest. By stilling his heart to hear the world’s quiet whispers, he has mastered the art of patient reflection, teaching his companions that even the greatest obstacles yield to the gentle power of waiting and thoughtful stillness.

characters.name: Baby Robot
characters.description: A small robot made of electronics and LEDs who goes on an adventure for honey.

characters.name: Mama Robot
characters.description: The baby robot's mother who waits for her at their multi-colored metal house.

characters.name: Dada Robot
characters.description: The baby robot's father who is happy to see his child return home.

places.name: Cornell Street
places.description: Bisected by a stark yellow line, this celestial artery cuts through a dense forest to chart a path toward the Mookers' planet. A cosmic thoroughfare where the infinite is stitched into the earthly, it anchors local commerce—typified by a towering tree of honey—alongside the residence of Uncle X, the house of the surreal bedroom feast, and Hattie’s home. It remains the vital threshold where the mundane pulse of the woods meets the stars, marking the point where Hattie begins her journey to find medical help.

places.name: Winnie the Pooh's House
places.description: The home where Pooh keeps his useful tall ladder.

places.name: The Robot Family House
places.description: A vibrant, multi-colored metal house decorated with leaves and gnomes.
